data.event_date: Soft opening: this week; grand opening early May (year not specified)
data.status: targeting a soft opening this week, followed by a grand opening in early May
data.short_description: Unhinged Coffee & Co., owned by Jonny Mocha and Cody Alexander Vaughn, is opening its first brick-and-mortar location at 319 N First Ave in Knightdale, transitioning from a mobile coffee cart operation. The day-to-night concept will serve espresso drinks, baked goods, beer, wine, and THC beverages, with a grand opening targeted for early May.

data.event_date: Approximately 2 months from article date (not specified)
data.status: targeting an opening in approximately two months
data.short_description: Vic's Italian Restaurant, owned by Mario Longo Jr., is opening a new fast-casual location at 5434 Six Forks Rd in Raleigh, taking over a standalone building previously occupied by KFC and Taco Bell. The roughly 75–80-seat restaurant will feature a drive-thru, semi-private dining area, beer and wine service, and a menu of Italian staples including pizzas, pasta, chicken parmesan, and baked ziti.

data.location: 29 West Park Square NE, Marietta, GA 30060
data.status: targeting an opening the week of April 20
data.short_description: West Park Sports Club, a Fork U Concepts venture led by Alexis and Scott Kinsey, is targeting an opening the week of April 20 in Marietta Square. The upscale two-level sports bar will feature extensive TV coverage, a separate downstairs bar, patio seating, and an elevated bar fare menu with items like oysters, wings, and burgers.

data.location: 2850 Paces Ferry Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339
data.event_date: Several months from article date (not specified)
data.status: will likely open in several months
data.short_description: Cotto Italian, the second location of a Fork U Concepts brand led by Alexis and Scott Kinsey, is taking over the former Stockyard space in Vinings and will open in several months after front-of-house renovations. The upscale Italian concept will mirror the menu and service style of its Gainesville location, offering lunch and dinner service on weekends and dinner during the week.

data.status: slated to open on April 16th
data.short_description: Surcheros Fresh Mex is slated to open at 1102 Trust Ave, Ste 300 in Augusta on April 16, owned by franchisee Madison Tuten who also operates a Surcheros on Washington Road in Augusta. The fast-casual Tex-Mex concept serves burritos, quesadillas, nachos, and more, with Tuten also planning additional locations in Evans, GA and Aiken, SC in Q1 2027.

data.location: Address not specified (mobile trailer serving metro Atlanta areas including West End, Atlantic Station, Piedmont Park, and Grant Park)
data.status: targeting a May 1 opening, with a potential soft opening ahead of that date
data.short_description: CocoLuxe Ice Cream & Sweets, owned by Tonya Whitfield, is a vegan, dairy-free dessert trailer targeting a May 1 launch across metro Atlanta. The concept will offer clean-label frozen treats including organic fruit-based pops and non-dairy ice cream, with plans to expand into scooped vegan ice cream and customizable bowls.

data.location: 1048 Copperfield Blvd NE, Suite 101, Concord, NC 28025
data.event_date: Soft opening mid-May; grand opening late May or early June (year not specified)
data.status: targeting a soft opening in mid-May, followed by a grand opening in late May or early June
data.short_description: Iron Oak Cigar Lounge, owned by Bjorn Mohaugen, is under construction at 1048 Copperfield Blvd NE in Concord and targeting a mid-May soft opening followed by a grand opening in late May or early June. The upscale concept will feature a bar with a focus on bourbon and cocktails, approximately 150 cigar SKUs in the humidor, and a golf simulator in the back of the venue.

data.location: 10610 Centrum Pkwy, Suite L, Pineville, NC 28134
data.event_date: Approximately 1–1.5 months from article date (not specified)
data.status: targeting an opening within about one to one-and-a-half months
data.short_description: Taste of Bengal, owned by Sabyasachi Patra, is preparing to open a Bengali-focused takeout, catering, and halal butcher concept at 10610 Centrum Pkwy in Pineville, pending remaining buildout progress. The concept will offer combo meals, biryani, curries, sweets, savory snacks, and a butcher section with frozen fish, meat, and chicken.

data.event_date: Late spring to early summer (year not specified)
data.status: targeting a late spring to early summer opening
data.short_description: Shaka Taco, co-owned by Steve Christian and Cody Leutgens, is preparing to open a new quick-service location at 4502 Fountain Drive in Wilmington near UNCW, currently undergoing kitchen expansion and construction. The coastal taco concept will offer Baja fish tacos, shrimp tacos, burritos, and bowls with fresh seafood and house-made sauces, with limited indoor seating and a covered outdoor porch.

data.status: set to open on October 29, 2026
data.short_description: Amorino is set to open at 7125 Medley Crossing in Johns Creek on October 29, 2026, marking the brand's third Georgia location as part of the larger Medley mixed-use development. Franchisee German Rodriguez will operate the gelato shop, which will offer gelato, gelato-filled macarons, crepes, waffles, coffee, and affogatos with both indoor and outdoor seating.

data.event_date: Mid-July to early August (year not specified)
data.status: anticipated opening window between mid-July and early August
data.short_description: Grain and Berry, a Florida-based superfood café franchise, is planning a new location at 2017 Creekside Landing Dr in Apex, operated by franchisee Sudarshan Krishnamurthi and currently in the pre-construction phase with permitting and contractor selection underway. The menu will center on açaí bowls, pitaya bowls, smoothies, juices, and other health-focused offerings, with the buildout expected to take approximately three to four months.

data.location: 930 Blanding Blvd., Suite D & E, Orange Park, FL 32065
data.status: target opening in early May
data.short_description: Gochas Latin Food, owned by Katzumi and Juan Yonekura, is preparing to open a 16-seat fast-casual Venezuelan restaurant at 930 Blanding Blvd in Orange Park, building on the success of their existing Gochas Empanadas food truck. The made-from-scratch menu will center on empanadas, cachapas, fresh juices, and breakfast offerings, with the kitchen led by Sonia Barajas.

data.location: 12553 Bartram Park Blvd., Suite 302, Jacksonville, FL 32258
data.status: targeting a June 18 opening, pending permits and construction
data.short_description: Lucky Goat Coffee, operated by franchisees Daniel and Nicole Price, is targeting a June 18 opening at 12553 Bartram Park Blvd in Jacksonville, pending permits and construction. The specialty coffee concept will feature a fast-casual format with 25–30 indoor seats, a small patio, an Italian-style espresso program, cold brew, and nitro cold brew options.

data.location: 3009 Ponce de Leon Blvd., Coral Gables, FL 33134
data.event_date: April 28 (year not specified); plans to be open by May 1
data.status: targeting an April 28 opening, with plans to be open by May 1
data.short_description: Five Iron Golf, led by Peter McCormick and partners Alex Zega and Matt Csillag, is targeting an April 28 opening at 3009 Ponce de Leon Blvd in Coral Gables as the group's Florida flagship. The indoor golf simulator, bar, and restaurant concept will feature approximately 175 seats across simulator bays, bowling lanes, and bar/dining areas, and will also introduce Strike Club, a sister concept with 12 full-size bowling lanes.

data.event_date: Approximately 2 weeks from article date (not specified)
data.status: targeting an opening in about two weeks
data.short_description: Goofy Foot Taproom and Brewery, owned by Jeff Thompson, is preparing to open at 523 West King Street in Boone, marking the brand's first expansion beyond its original High Point taproom that opened in 2018. The two-floor venue will feature 16–18 rotating taps focused on NC breweries, live music, trivia, THC slushies, a full cocktail bar in the basement, and snack items.

data.location: 1246 Central Ave., Suite 104, St. Petersburg, FL 33705
data.status: set to open on May 2, with a grand opening event planned from 1 p.m.–3 p.m.
data.short_description: Amorino is set to open at 1246 Central Ave., Suite 104 in St. Petersburg on May 2 with a grand opening event featuring free gelato, marking the ninth location for partners Narmine Dakak, Naser Dakak, and German Rodriguez and their second in Florida. The shop will offer handcrafted rose-shaped gelato cones, an Italian espresso bar, crepes, waffles, and affogatos with approximately 15–20 indoor and patio seats.

data.location: 2263 Cheshire Bridge Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30324
data.status: recently opened (soft opens)
data.short_description: French Toast Buckhead has officially opened at 2263 Cheshire Bridge Rd NE in Atlanta, taking over the space formerly occupied by Blu Lagoon, as of April 6, 2026. The breakfast, brunch, and lunch concept from Marietta offers French toast variations, beef short ribs bowls, curry goat bowls, and breakfast sandwiches, operating seven days a week.

data.location: 122 N. Dixie Highway, West Palm Beach, FL 33401
data.status: Now open
data.short_description: Brighten Your Day Cafe, led by Chef Ethan Haim, is now open at 122 N. Dixie Highway in West Palm Beach as a fast-casual breakfast and lunch café with counter service and indoor/outdoor seating. The menu includes egg sandwiches, omelets, Benedicts, smoothies, specialty French toast, wraps, burgers, and house-made soups.

data.location: 8801 Boynton Beach Blvd, Boynton Beach, FL 33472
data.status: Now open
data.short_description: Bud's Chicken & Seafood, owned by Phillip Brinkman and family, is now open at 8801 Boynton Beach Blvd in Boynton Beach as a fast-casual concept focused on made-to-order fried chicken and seafood. The menu includes fresh chicken tenders, fried cod, shrimp, clams, and house-made sides.

data.status: Now open
data.short_description: Due Cuori, owned by Monique Rabyor and Alfredo Verrone, is now open at 405 Federal Highway in Lake Park as a full-service Italian American restaurant with a casual, homey setting and indoor/outdoor seating. The menu features house-made pasta, cacio e pepe, burrata, antipasti, and classic Italian dishes.

data.location: 1649 Forum Place, Suite 3, West Palm Beach, FL 33401
data.status: Now open
data.short_description: Bona Pizza Pasta 2 is now open at 1649 Forum Place, Suite 3 in West Palm Beach as a casual Italian-American restaurant offering dine-in and takeout. The menu includes pizza, wings, baked pastas, and entrees like chicken francese and veal.

data.status: targeting a Q1 2027 opening, pending infrastructure work by the city
data.short_description: Culver's is planning a new approximately 4,500 sq ft location at 1921 US-92 in Auburndale, led by co-owner Nathan Devine and owner-operator Scott Hunt, targeting a Q1 2027 opening pending city infrastructure work. The restaurant will follow the brand's standard format with dine-in, drive-thru, and roughly 75 seats, offering ButterBurgers, chicken, seafood, fresh frozen custard, and Wisconsin cheese curds.

data.location: Main Street, Horseshoe Beach, FL (no zip code specified)
data.status: set to open Tuesday, April 7 at 11 a.m.
data.short_description: Tides Turning Food Trailer, owned and operated by Jill and Tyler Futch, is set to open April 7 on Main Street in Horseshoe Beach, marking a return to the food industry after hurricane-related setbacks including the closure of their previous restaurant, The Shrimp Boat. The menu will feature seafood sandwiches, shrimp burgers, coconut shrimp rice bowls, and Saturday breakfast items, with occasional appearances at local festivals.

data.status: targeting an early May opening
data.short_description: Salty Bagel and Grill, owned by Bernard and Elizabeth Corrigan, is targeting an early May opening at 23 W Hibiscus Blvd in Melbourne, marking the family-run brand's sixth location. The bagel concept offers 24 daily bagel flavors, hot and cold sandwiches, cream cheese options, and an espresso coffee program, with approximately 15 indoor seats and potential outdoor seating.

data.event_date: Not specified (soft opening within 3–4 weeks of article)
data.status: soft opening expected in approximately three to four weeks
data.short_description: Pho 5 Spices is a new Vietnamese restaurant from owners Tobey and Jenny Vu currently completing interior renovations. The menu will focus on pho, noodle dishes, and rice plates in a 45–56 seat full-service space. It will operate seven days a week serving lunch and dinner.

data.status: targeting an April 11 reopening following its remodel
data.short_description: 45 Sports Bar & Lounge closed for roughly four to five months to renovate, upgrade equipment, and refine the menu before relaunching. The updated concept will focus on Puerto Rican cuisine including tripleta sandwiches and mofongo in a reworked space seating approximately 123 guests. Owner Roberto Anglada invested in new equipment and a refreshed layout to reopen in a stronger form.

data.status: targeting a grand opening on April 29th
data.short_description: The Wine Bar is a boutique upscale concept from the Surapine family featuring approximately 178 globally sourced wines, a self-serve dispenser system, and a food program of charcuterie, Mediterranean-style plates, and small bites. Construction began in December and the team is completing final interior details. The space includes private booth-style nooks, a glass-enclosed tasting room, and varied seating throughout.

data.event_date: Not specified (approximately one month from article)
data.status: targeting an opening in about a month
data.short_description: Casa Cotzelli is a full-service Italian restaurant from partners Filippo Butelli and Gabriele Cozza, marking their second location following a North Naples debut three years prior. The menu will feature made-from-scratch pasta, sauces, and regional Italian dishes including eggplant parmesan, paccheri with pistachio pesto, and a Fiorentina steak. The space is expected to seat 120–125 guests with an open kitchen and both indoor and outdoor dining.

data.location: 1400 E Hillsboro Blvd, Unit 101, Deerfield Beach, FL 33441
data.status: Coming Soon (targeting May; pending final permits)
data.short_description: X7 Eatery is a 25-seat casual concept offering dishes from multiple Asian cuisines under one roof, led by Chef Jiaren "Carlos" Zheng. The menu includes sushi, Korean fried chicken, Japanese sandwiches, wagyu, dry-aged items, and handmade dumplings. It is planned to operate daily from 11 a.m. to late.

data.event_date: Targeting approximately five months from last update
data.status: Coming Soon (targeting opening in approximately five months from last update)
data.short_description: Poke I Love You is a larger second location with expanded seating for gatherings operating in a fast-casual format with dine-in and takeout. The menu includes customizable poke bowls, bubble tea, ramen, and Korean corn dogs. Owner is Brian Cosar.

data.status: Coming Soon (targeting July; pending approvals)
data.short_description: Midori is a 50-seat Japanese restaurant with a sushi counter, private dining, and patio seating focused on quality-driven dining. The menu includes sushi, temaki, rice and soba bowls, and bento boxes with seafood sourced from Japan. Owner is Álvaro Perez Miranda.

data.status: Now Open
data.short_description: Chef Reece Kitchen is an expanded Jamaican fusion restaurant with about 70 seats operating in a counter-service format with dine-in and catering. The menu features oxtail, jerk chicken, honey garlic salmon, pasta variations, and lunch specials.

data.location: 801 N Federal Hwy, Suite 108, Hallandale Beach, FL 33009
data.event_date: Soft opening February 15; Grand opening March 21
data.status: now open
data.short_description: Oakberry opened in Atlantic Village under franchisees Ademar and Christiane Guedin, marking their first location with a second already in the works. The fast, health-focused concept specializes in customizable açaí bowls, smoothies, juices, and functional beverages in an approximately 750 sq ft space. The soft opening began February 15 and the grand opening event was held March 21.

data.status: Targeting a May 1 opening
data.short_description: Big Jake's BBQ is a fast-casual barbecue spot with a drive-thru and limited indoor seating focused on quick service and takeout, from owners Robert Rukab and partner Zee. The menu includes pit-cooked chicken, ribs, and classic sides like mac and cheese, beans, potato salad, and coleslaw. It is planned to operate daily from 10 a.m. to 10 p.m.

data.status: Targeting a June opening (second local location)
data.short_description: Potbelly Sandwich Works is a Chicago-born sandwich chain known for toasted subs in a casual setting with indoor seating and patio space, expanding via franchisees Patrick and Leah Pendergast with multiple locations planned across Northeast Florida. The menu features signature subs, milkshakes, soups, mac and cheese, salads, and fresh-baked cookies. Hours are expected daily from 9 a.m. to 10 p.m.

data.event_date: Targeting late spring to early summer
data.status: Targeting a late spring to early summer opening
data.short_description: Kappuccinos is a dual-concept space operating as a breakfast café by day and a sports bar by night, built as a neighborhood gathering spot by owners Keith and Catherine Jones. The breakfast menu includes eggs, bacon, and shrimp and grits, while the evening menu features burgers, cheesesteaks, wings, oysters, and steak dinners. Hours are planned Tuesday–Sunday with breakfast 6 a.m.–1 p.m. and sports bar 5–10 p.m.

data.store_name: Dunc's Bar and Grill featuring Southern Yankee Queen
data.location: 196 Miracle Strip Pkwy SE Unit H, Fort Walton Beach, FL 32548
data.status: targeting an early May opening
data.short_description: Dunc's Bar and Grill combines a neighborhood bar with Southern comfort food led by Beatrice Stjulien of Southern Yankee Queen, which was previously a catering business. The menu includes po'boys, Reubens, wings, oxtails, meatloaf, collard greens, and baked macaroni and cheese in a fully remodeled space. The restaurant was initially targeting an April opening but shifted to the first week of May pending final approvals.

non_working.reason: Partial text: The roundup article text is cut off mid-table; only four of the six advertised Palm Beach County businesses (Brighten Your Day Cafe, Bud's Chicken & Seafood, Due Cuori, and Bona Pizza Pasta 2) had sufficient detail to extract. The remaining two businesses referenced in the title were not present in the provided text.
